program_name: MSS CRC Immunotherapy Core Failure Corpus
active_engagement: Brenus Pharma (BreAK CRC-001, STC-1010) — fit score 0.7375

trials.primary_result: OS HR 0.73 (90% CI 0.55–0.97, p=0.07); median OS 6.6 vs 4.1 months

D1_biology.rationale: PD-L1+CTLA-4 combination is biologically sound — dual checkpoint blockade.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Unselected MSS CRC population. pTMB ≥28 subgroup OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022) — the responder subgroup exists but was diluted. Liver-met patients (HR=1.39) were enrolled alongside NLM patients (HR=0.54), canceling the signal.

D6_interpretability.rationale: pTMB ≥28 threshold was data-driven (not pre-specified) — requires prospective validation.

responder_subpopulations.note: Strongest IO signal from randomized data in MSS CRC
IO is HARMFUL in liver-met patients

trials.transfer_lesson: Apply BOTH gates simultaneously: pTMB ≥28 AND non-liver-metastatic. The intersection of these two gates defines the IO-responsive subpopulation in MSS CRC.

trials.drug: Atezolizumab (PD-L1) + Cobimetinib (MEKi) vs Atezolizumab vs Regorafenib
trials.primary_result: OS: atezo+cobi 8.87 months vs atezo 7.10 months vs rego 8.51 months — no significant difference

D1_biology.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. MEK inhibition definitively fails to increase tumor immunogenicity in clinical MSS CRC. The preclinical hypothesis (MEKi upregulates MHC-I, reduces immunosuppressive cytokines) did not translate. Additionally, cobimetinib suppresses T cell proliferation — the combination may be antagonistic.

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected population — but even with selection, MEK inhibition is not a viable IO sensitization strategy.

D7_translational.rationale: Preclinical-to-clinical translation failure — MEKi immunogenicity effects seen in mouse models did not replicate in humans.

trials.transfer_lesson: MEK inhibition is NOT a viable IO sensitization strategy in MSS CRC. Any abstract proposing MEK+IO in MSS CRC is repeating a definitively failed hypothesis. Do not pursue.

trials.primary_result: ORR 0% (0/22 evaluable). Confirmed T-cell responses to vaccine antigens in peripheral blood.

D1_biology.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Pharmacodynamic dissociation — T cells activated in periphery but cannot kill tumor cells. The vaccine mechanism is biologically sound (T-cell activation confirmed) but the TME barriers prevent tumor killing. This is the canonical pharmacodynamic dissociation failure.

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected population — but even with selection, the stromal barrier problem remains.

D6_interpretability.rationale: T-cell responses confirmed but ORR 0% — the disconnect between immune activation and tumor regression is the key finding.

trials.key_finding: T-cell activation ≠ tumor regression in MSS CRC. The stromal barrier is the missing piece.
trials.transfer_lesson: Vaccine programs in MSS CRC must address the stromal barrier (TGF-β, CAFs) to convert immune activation into tumor killing. Vaccine alone is insufficient.

trials.primary_result: ITT PFS HR 0.69 (p=0.0075) — driven by MSI-H patients. pMMR subgroup PFS HR 0.86 (95% CI 0.63–1.17) — NOT significant

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. ITT benefit driven by MSI-H patients (dMMR). pMMR (MSS) subgroup shows no benefit (HR 0.86, not significant). Immunoscore-IC high subgroup shows directional benefit (HR ~0.50) — the immune-inflamed subpopulation within MSS CRC can benefit.

D1_biology.rationale: Atezolizumab mechanism is sound — PD-L1 blockade. The biology works in the right patient.

responder_subpopulations.note: Positive control — IO works in MSI-H
Unselected MSS CRC does not benefit

trials.transfer_lesson: In 1L MSS CRC, IO benefit requires Immunoscore-IC high (immune-inflamed TME). Unselected pMMR patients do not benefit. Liver metastasis interaction: AtezoTRIBE 2025 update shows Pint PFS=0.166 — liver metastasis is not predictive when Immunoscore-IC is high.

trials.drug: NANT vaccine cocktail + Nivolumab + mFOLFOX6 + Bevacizumab
trials.primary_result: PFS HR 1.061 (95% CI 0.380–2.966, p=0.91) — TERMINATED for futility

D1_biology.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Pharmacodynamic dissociation — vaccine activates T cells but cannot overcome MSS CRC TME barriers. The combination of vaccine + IO + chemo + bev is mechanistically complex but the fundamental problem (immune exclusion in MSS CRC) is not addressed.

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected MSS CRC — no liver metastasis stratification, no pTMB gate.

D3_architecture.rationale: Trial design precedent: adding IO to mFOLFOX6+bev in unselected MSS CRC does not improve PFS. This is the design precedent for BreAK CRC-001.

trials.transfer_lesson: QUILT-2.004 is the design precedent for BreAK CRC-001. Adding IO to mFOLFOX6+bev in unselected MSS CRC does not work. Patient selection (liver metastasis, pTMB) is mandatory.

trials.primary_result: HR 0.73 (90% CI 0.44–1.21) — CI crosses 1.0; data immature at bankruptcy. ctDNA-low subgroup HR 0.50

D2_selection.rationale: ctDNA-low subgroup HR 0.50 — directional benefit. ctDNA-low patients have lower disease burden and more intact immune function. This is a proxy for the immune-inflamed subpopulation.

D8_systemic.rationale: Gritstone Bio filed Chapter 11 October 2024 — data immature at bankruptcy. Results cannot be fully interpreted.

trials.transfer_lesson: ctDNA-low as a proxy for IO-responsive subpopulation in MSS CRC. Personalized neoantigen vaccines show directional benefit in ctDNA-low patients — this is the immune-inflamed subpopulation.

trials.primary_result: Median PFS 8.2 months (95% CI 5.9–8.6); ORR 64.5%; no randomized control arm

D3_architecture.rationale: PRIMARY LIMITATION. Single-arm design — cannot determine if IO adds benefit over mFOLFOX6 alone (TRIBE2 control arm: mPFS 9.8 months, ORR 50%). ORR 64.5% is higher than TRIBE2 ORR 50% but this may reflect patient selection (1L, good PS) rather than IO benefit.

D2_selection.rationale: CD8-high + TGF-β-low subgroup showed best outcomes — consistent with immune-inflamed hypothesis.

trials.transfer_lesson: MEDITREME ORR 64.5% vs TRIBE2 ORR 50% — directional signal but uncontrolled. The CD8-high + TGF-β-low subgroup finding supports the immune-inflamed hypothesis.

what_crispro_proved: THREE CONDITIONS for IO benefit in MSS CRC: non-liver-metastatic + pTMB ≥28 + immune-inflamed TME
CO.26: pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022) — strongest IO signal from randomized data
CO.26: liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (HARMFUL) vs no-LM HR=0.54; Pint=0.02
REGONIVO US: ORR 7% overall; 0/5 liver-met responders; non-liver-met ORR 22%
GVAX: ORR 0% despite confirmed T-cell responses — pharmacodynamic dissociation
IMblaze370 (Phase III): OS no significant difference — MEK inhibition definitively fails
AtezoTRIBE: pMMR PFS HR=0.86 (NOT significant); Immunoscore-IC high HR ~0.50 (exploratory)
QUILT-2.004: HR 1.061 (p=0.91) — IO addition to mFOLFOX6+bev fails in unselected MSS CRC
